differential_diagnoses: Paraneoplastic limbic encephalitis (e.g., anti-LGI1 or anti-Hu)
Alzheimer's disease with comorbid cerebral amyloid angiopathy
Primary angiitis of the central nervous system (PACNS)
next_steps: Send cerebrospinal fluid for autoimmune/paraneoplastic encephalitis panel
Initiate empiric high-dose intravenous glucocorticoids for suspected inflammatory/autoimmune process
Perform whole-body PET-CT to evaluate for occult malignancy
Complete video-EEG monitoring to characterize epileptiform activity
Obtain amyloid PET scan or CSF biomarkers for Alzheimer's disease
Evaluate for specific antibodies associated with limbic encephalitis (LGI1, CASPR2, Hu, Ma2)

differential_diagnoses: Paraneoplastic limbic encephalitis (e.g., anti-Hu or anti-Ma2)
Alzheimer's disease with comorbid cerebral amyloid angiopathy
Primary angiitis of the central nervous system (PACNS)
next_steps: Send cerebrospinal fluid for comprehensive autoimmune and paraneoplastic encephalitis antibody panel including LGI1, CASPR2, Hu, Ma2, and NMDA
Perform whole-body 18F-FDG PET-CT to evaluate for occult malignancy or metabolic signatures of encephalitis
Initiate empiric treatment with high-dose intravenous methylprednisolone for suspected autoimmune encephalitis
Continue aggressive antiseizure therapy with close video-EEG monitoring to manage subclinical seizure activity
Perform repeat brain MRI with contrast to evaluate for new leptomeningeal or parenchymal enhancement
Obtain CSF biomarkers for Alzheimer's disease (Aβ42/40 ratio, p-tau) to clarify neurodegenerative component
